question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any validation in getTopic() that the customFee's entityId matches the topic's id, causing a scenario where a database join or lookup error returns a custom fee belonging to a different entity, leading clients to freeze funds by paying fees to an unintended fee collector account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the getTopic() endpoint does not check whether the topic's fee_schedule_key is null (meaning fees cannot be changed), causing clients to incorrectly assume fees are mutable and hold funds in reserve for anticipated fee changes that will never occur, permanently freezing those reserved funds?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getTopic() does not expose the topic's submit_key in a way that clients can verify whether they are authorized to submit messages, causing clients to freeze funds by pre-paying custom fees for message submissions that will be permanently rejected due to missing submit key authorization?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the topicMapper.map() function maps the customFee object directly without checking whether the fee collector account specified in the custom fee is frozen for the token used to pay the fee, causing clients to freeze funds by attempting to pay fees in a token that the fee collector cannot receive?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that EntityIdNumParameter uses a regex that allows up to 12 digits for the num component, submitting a num value that is valid per the regex but exceeds the maximum valid entity number on the network, causing the endpoint to return data for an entity that should not exist and whose custom fee schedule could cause clients to permanently freeze funds?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the getTopic() endpoint does not validate that the topic is in an active (non-deleted, non-expired) state before returning its custom fee schedule, causing clients to freeze funds by paying fees to submit messages to a topic that has been deleted and will permanently reject all submissions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the independent calls to topicService.findById() and customFeeService.findById() in getTopic() to receive a response where the topic data reflects a post-fee-update state but the custom fee data reflects a pre-fee-update state (or vice versa), causing clients to freeze funds by paying fees based on a fee schedule that was never simultaneously valid with the returned topic state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the getTopic() endpoint returns the autoRenewAccount field mapped from entity.autoRenewAccountId without checking if that account is frozen or deleted, causing clients to fund the autoRenewAccount for topic renewal, only for the renewal to fail permanently because the autoRenewAccount cannot receive funds, effectively freezing the renewal funds?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that customFeeService.findById() always throws EntityNotFoundException when no custom fee exists for a topic, making it impossible to distinguish between a topic with no fees and a topic whose fee data is missing from the mirror node, causing clients to assume all queryable topics have fees and freeze funds by pre-paying fees that don't exist?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the getTopic() endpoint does not include any on-chain verification of the returned custom fee data, causing clients to trust mirror node data that may be corrupted or manipulated at the database level, leading to permanent fund freezing by paying fees to incorrect fee collector accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the getTopic() endpoint does not expose the topic's memo field in a way that would warn clients about fee changes or topic deprecation, causing clients to continue paying fees for a topic that has been effectively abandoned by its owner, permanently freezing funds in fee payments that serve no purpose?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the EntityIdNumParameter regex allows leading zeros in the num component (e.g., 0.0.001000), causing the parsed entity ID to resolve to a different entity than the user intended if the system normalizes or does not normalize leading zeros consistently, returning wrong custom fee data and causing clients to freeze funds?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the getTopic() endpoint does not validate that the fee collector account specified in the returned custom fee is an active, unfrozen account capable of receiving the fee token, causing clients to freeze funds by paying fees that will be permanently rejected because the fee collector account is frozen for the relevant token?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the getTopic() endpoint does not expose the topic's expiration_time field prominently enough for clients to check before paying fees, causing clients to freeze funds by paying custom fees to submit messages to a topic that will expire before the messages are processed?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the topicMapper.map() function maps entity.timestampRange to timestamp without validating that the timestamp range is current (i.e., the upper bound is null or in the future), causing clients to receive fee data for a historical snapshot of the topic and freeze funds by paying fees based on an outdated fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the getTopic() endpoint does not check whether the custom fee's fee_collector_account_id has been deleted on-chain, causing clients to freeze funds by paying fees to a deleted account that can never be recovered, resulting in permanent fund loss?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the getTopic() endpoint does not expose whether the topic requires a submit_key signature, causing clients to freeze funds by paying custom fees for message submissions that will be permanently rejected due to missing submit key authorization, with no way to recover the paid fees?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the getTopic() endpoint does not validate that the custom fee's denominating token (for fractional or royalty fees) is not frozen for the payer, causing clients to freeze funds by attempting to pay fees in a token for which they are frozen, resulting in permanently rejected transactions and frozen funds?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the getTopic() endpoint does not expose the topic's admin_key field, causing clients to be unable to determine whether the topic's fee schedule can be modified, leading them to freeze funds in anticipation of fee changes that are impossible because the admin key is null?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the getTopic() endpoint does not include any warning when the topic's auto-renew period is about to expire and the autoRenewAccount has insufficient balance, causing clients to freeze funds by paying custom fees for message submissions to a topic that will expire before the messages are processed?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the getTopic() endpoint does not validate that the custom fee amounts are within the network's maximum fee limits, causing clients to receive and act on fee data that specifies amounts exceeding what the network will accept, leading to permanently rejected transactions and frozen funds?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the getTopic() endpoint does not expose the topic's deleted field in a way that prevents clients from paying fees for a deleted topic, causing clients to freeze funds by paying custom fees for message submissions that will be permanently rejected because the topic has been deleted?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the getTopic() endpoint does not validate that the custom fee's fee_collector_account_id is associated with the correct token (for token-denominated fees), causing clients to freeze funds by paying fees in the wrong token that will be permanently rejected by the network?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the getTopic() endpoint does not expose the topic's running_hash or sequence_number, causing clients to be unable to verify the integrity of the topic state before paying fees, leading to permanent fund freezing by paying fees for a topic whose state has been corrupted in the mirror node?
